what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG icon that draws an unfilled rounded square outline using the current text color.
observations: Root svg sets xmlns, viewBox, fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-linecap, and stroke-linejoin attributes.
The icon contains a single rect element at x=4 and y=4 with width=16, height=16, and rx=2.
The stroke uses currentColor, so the icon color is inherited from its rendering context.
The SVG has no title, desc, role, id, class, or inline dimensions.
conventions: Uses rounded line caps and joins.
Uses a 1.5 stroke width for a thin outline icon style.
concerns: No embedded accessibility metadata; labeling depends on the consuming context.
notes: A minimal SVG asset for rendering an unchecked checkbox state.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be embedded in the SVG or handled by the consumer?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates an empty checkbox icon, and the file renders an unfilled checkbox-like rounded square.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a neutral unchecked checkbox icon that adapts to surrounding color and sizing contexts.
contribution: Provides a reusable, color-inheriting empty checkbox outline icon for interfaces that need an unchecked control state visual.
summary_for_embedding: A compact 24x24 SVG asset containing one stroked rounded rectangle that represents an empty checkbox.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for supplying a reusable unchecked checkbox icon in a design-system icon set.
